label: Uncomplicated tendinitis without rupture or infection concern
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated tendinitis/tendonitis.
No tendon rupture concern, infection, open wound, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Tendon rupture concern, inability to move/use limb, infection, open wound, fever, systemic illness, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, compartment syndrome, or high-energy trauma.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed orthopedic/sports medicine plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, occupational health, or orthopedics if function is not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing as tendon inflammation/irritation with pain near a joint and supportive care such as rest, activity modification, and clinician-directed pain control. Autonomous Phase 1851-1900 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated tendinitis/tendonitis without rupture concern, infection, trauma/fracture concern, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing as tendon inflammation/irritation with pain near a joint and supportive care such as rest, activity modification, and clinician-directed pain control. Autonomous Phase 1851-1900 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated tendinitis/tendonitis without rupture concern, infection, trauma/fracture concern, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated tendinitis/tendonitis
no rupture, infection, or neurovascular red flags documented
no trauma/fracture concern, high-energy trauma, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway
